annotations.label: Catenary as graph of cosh

anchor.snippet: the catenary curve is the graph of the hyperbolic cosine function

annotations.note: Mathlib defines Real.cosh but has no notion of the catenary curve as the graph of a cosh.

annotations.label: Catenoid is a minimal surface of revolution

anchor.snippet: The surface of revolution of the catenary curve, the catenoid , is a minimal surface

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of minimal surface or surface of revolution, let alone the catenoid.

annotations.label: Hanging chain minimizes potential energy

anchor.snippet: A hanging chain will assume a shape of least potential energy which is a catenary

annotations.note: No formalization of hanging chains or potential energy minimization for catenaries exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flattened catenary

anchor.snippet: a more general curve called a flattened catenary, with equation y = A cosh( Bx )

annotations.note: Mathlib has no catenary or flattened catenary curve definition.

annotations.label: Cartesian equation of the catenary

anchor.snippet: The equation of a catenary in Cartesian coordinates has the form

annotations.note: No catenary equation y = a cosh(x/a) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: All catenaries are similar

anchor.snippet: All catenary curves are similar to each other, since changing the parameter a is equivalent to a uniform scaling of the curve

annotations.note: No catenary curve family exists in Mathlib, so similarity among them is not stated.

annotations.label: Whewell equation of the catenary

anchor.snippet: The Whewell equation for the catenary is

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Whewell equation or intrinsic curve equation for the catenary.

annotations.label: Cesàro equation of the catenary

anchor.snippet: eliminating [MATH] gives the Cesàro equation

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Cesàro (intrinsic) equation for a plane curve, much less for the catenary.

annotations.label: Radius of curvature equals normal length

anchor.snippet: which is the length of the normal between the curve and the x -axis

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks plane-curve radius of curvature and normal-segment-length machinery.

annotations.label: Roulette of parabola focus is a catenary

anchor.snippet: When a parabola is rolled along a straight line, the roulette curve traced by its focus is a catenary

annotations.note: Mathlib has no roulette construction or parabola/catenary geometry.

annotations.label: Envelope of parabola directrix is a catenary

anchor.snippet: The envelope of the directrix of the parabola is also a catenary

annotations.note: Mathlib has no envelope-of-curves theory or this catenary characterization.

annotations.label: Involute of catenary from vertex is the tractrix

anchor.snippet: The involute from the vertex, that is the roulette traced by a point starting at the vertex when a line is rolled on a catenary, is the tractrix

annotations.note: Neither involutes of curves, the catenary, nor the tractrix appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rolling line on catenary gives another line

anchor.snippet: Another roulette, formed by rolling a line on a catenary, is another line

annotations.note: Mathlib has no rolling-curve/roulette construction.

annotations.label: Square wheels roll on inverted catenaries

anchor.snippet: square wheels can roll perfectly smoothly on a road made of a series of bumps in the shape of an inverted catenary curve

annotations.note: Not in Mathlib; no rolling-polygon geometry exists.

annotations.label: Area-to-length ratio characterizes the catenary

anchor.snippet: Over any horizontal interval, the ratio of the area under the catenary to its length equals a , independent of the interval selected

annotations.note: No catenary geometry in Mathlib; the area/arclength ratio identity is not stated.

annotations.label: Catenary is unique with constant area-to-length ratio

anchor.snippet: The catenary is the only plane curve other than a horizontal line with this property

annotations.note: The characterization theorem is absent—no catenary or area-to-arclength identity in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centroid of catenary region

anchor.snippet: the geometric centroid of the area under a stretch of catenary is the midpoint of the perpendicular segment connecting the centroid of the curve itself and the x -axis

annotations.note: Catenary centroid result is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Charge in uniform electric field traces a catenary

anchor.snippet: A moving charge in a uniform electric field travels along a catenary

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize classical electrodynamics or charge trajectories.

annotations.label: Catenoid is the minimum surface of revolution

anchor.snippet: The surface of revolution with fixed radii at either end that has minimum surface area is a catenary

annotations.note: No surface-of-revolution or minimal-area variational result in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Differential equation of the catenary from force balance

anchor.snippet: A differential equation for the curve may be derived as follows

annotations.note: The mechanical derivation of the catenary ODE is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Horizontal and vertical components of tension

anchor.snippet: The horizontal component of the tension, T cos φ = T 0 is constant and the vertical component of the tension, T sin φ = ws is proportional to the length of chain between r and the vertex

annotations.note: Statics of hanging cables is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Derivation of the catenary equation

anchor.section: Derivation of equations for the curve
anchor.snippet: can be solved to produce equations for the curve

annotations.note: Catenary derivation is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Alternative derivation of the catenary

anchor.snippet: The differential equation can be solved using a different approach

annotations.note: No alternative catenary derivation is formalized.

annotations.label: Determining catenary parameters from endpoints and length

anchor.snippet: These data can be determined from the position of two given points

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize fitting a catenary to endpoints and arclength.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of equilibrium position

anchor.snippet: there is at most one solution with a > 0 and so there is at most one position of equilibrium

annotations.note: No uniqueness statement for catenary equilibrium exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Horizontal traction force formula

anchor.snippet: The horizontal traction force at P 1 and P 2 is T 0 = wa , where w is the weight per unit length of the chain or cable

annotations.note: Tension-force formula for hanging chains is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tension in terms of coordinates

anchor.snippet: There is a simple relationship between the tension in the cable at a point and its x - and/or y - coordinate

annotations.note: Catenary tension relations are not formalized.

annotations.label: Variational formulation of the catenary

anchor.snippet: The curve has to minimize its potential energy

annotations.note: Calculus-of-variations derivation of the catenary is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equations for nonuniform chains

anchor.snippet: If the density of the chain is variable then the analysis above can be adapted to produce equations for the curve given the density

annotations.note: Nonuniform hanging chain equations are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Suspension bridge cable follows a parabola

anchor.snippet: and so the cable follows a parabola

annotations.note: Mathlib has no suspension-bridge cable analysis or parabola characterization.

annotations.label: Catenary of equal strength

anchor.snippet: In a catenary of equal strength, the cable is strengthened according to the magnitude of the tension at each point, so its resistance to breaking is constant along its length

annotations.note: The catenary of equal strength is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Span of catenary of equal strength

anchor.snippet: the curve has vertical asymptotes and this limits the span to π c

annotations.note: No statements about the span of the catenary of equal strength in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elastic catenary

anchor.snippet: In an elastic catenary, the chain is replaced by a spring which can stretch in response to tension

annotations.note: Elastic catenary is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rigid limit of elastic catenary

anchor.snippet: At the rigid limit where E is large, the shape of the curve reduces to that of a non-elastic chain

annotations.note: No elastic-catenary limit analysis is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equilibrium equation for chain under general force

anchor.snippet: These equations can be used as the starting point in the analysis of a flexible chain acting under any external force

annotations.note: Flexible-chain equilibrium equations under general forces are absent from Mathlib.
